planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: CSV present in artifacts; formulas include the key computed cells (fraction_of_limit, pressure_penalty, vessel_treatment_risk_score, action cost formulas).
planned_changes.description: Add a 'Formulas' sheet to the workbook and export the same as a CSV (pfas_breakthrough_formulas.csv) listing each formula cell: sheet, cell_address, formula_text.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Memo file present and previewable; cost table shows per-action cost, any days assumptions, and total vs budget checked.
planned_changes.description: Produce the full, untruncated treatment_decision_memo_full.md that contains: explicit throttle_flow_days assumption, itemized per-action costs, cumulative cost arithmetic showing <= budget, and the final recommended action with rationale.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Validation excerpt present and demonstrates match_flag values; it references the packet row IDs used.
planned_changes.description: Export a Validation excerpt (validation_excerpt.csv) that shows packet_row_id | packet_field | packet_value | workbook_value | match_flag for representative rows.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Workbook contains packet_row_id and needs_review columns and these are referenced in memo.
planned_changes.description: Modify the workbook Packet_Data sheet to include an explicit 'packet_row_id' column and add visible 'needs_review' flags for assumption-driven action-effect cells.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: README present in attempt directory and referenced in memo or trace.
planned_changes.description: Add a short README_spreadsheet_generation.md describing the XML-based workbook generator, including a confirmation that formulas were written as formulas (not as precomputed numeric values), and how to reproduce the workbook generation step if needed.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Score aggregation method text present in memo or separate artifact.
planned_changes.description: Document score aggregation method (if retaining grader semantic scores) in the memo or a small Score_Audit note so the derivation of final_score is auditable.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline successfully produced the requested workbook and decision memo and the grader/verifier accepted the deliverables. However programmatic verification was limited because preview tooling could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo preview is truncated. The grader/verifier flagged three verification gaps that reduce auditability: (1) inability to programmatically inspect formulas in the workbook, (2) missing explicit cost-duration (throttle_flow_days) and a full cost breakdown in the memo, and (3) truncated memo preventing inspection of final action arithmetic and rationale. Addressing these will make the deliverable verifiable and reduce grader uncertainty.
